stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 crop selection options. The probability of favorable weather patterns is uncertain, estimated between 43% and 62%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: rice: $54K in good weather patterns, $12K in bad weather patterns. wheat: $280K in good weather patterns, $77K in bad weather patterns. soybeans: $204K in good weather patterns, $121K in bad weather patterns. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.53: best option is wheat with EV=$184.6K. wheat is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [164.3, 202.9].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
wheat is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
